excluded_defs.name: Vars
excluded_defs.reason: read: considered changing Vars() to return empty map on nil, but task says fix in regexp.go; no change needed in Vars() itself

excluded_defs.name: extractVars
excluded_defs.reason: read: already initializes output map when nil, but only called when there are variables; not the right fix point

exploration_log.aha_moment: The setMatch function only calls extractVars when varsN is non-empty, so routes without path variables never initialize m.Vars. The fix must initialize m.Vars at the top of setMatch, and then requestWithVars/requestWithRouteAndVars must use vars == nil (not len(vars) == 0) to propagate the empty map into context.

dead_ends.why_irrelevant: Could return empty map when rv is nil, but task specifies fix in regexp.go; changing Vars() alone doesn't fix the root cause

exploration_log.hindsight: Changing the `len(vars) == 0` guards to `vars == nil` is essential; without that change, an empty map from setMatch would still not reach the context.

alternatives: Fix only Vars() to return empty map instead of nil
Fix only setMatch to initialize m.Vars
key_decisions.decision: Fix setMatch to initialize m.Vars AND update requestWithVars/requestWithRouteAndVars to use vars == nil guard
key_decisions.reasoning: Just fixing setMatch without updating the nil checks in context helpers would still result in empty map not reaching context (len(empty map) == 0 skips context.WithValue)

search_sequence.action: Traced code path from Route.Match() through setMatch() to requestWithRouteAndVars() to Vars()
search_sequence.reasoning: Needed to understand full flow to identify all fix points
search_sequence.result: Confirmed: setMatch never initializes m.Vars for routes with no variables; requestWithRouteAndVars skips empty maps; Vars() returns nil

solve_notes: Three changes: (1) In regexp.go's setMatch, initialize m.Vars to make(map[string]string) at the top so routes with no variables always produce a non-nil map. (2) In mux.go's requestWithVars, change the guard from len(vars)==0 to vars==nil so an empty map is stored in context. (3) Same change in requestWithRouteAndVars. This ensures Vars(r) always returns a non-nil map after a successful match, preventing panics on iteration.
task_text:
Fix Vars() returning nil instead of empty map for routes without path variables

When a route matches but has no path variables (e.g., a static path
`/health`), `mux.Vars(r)` returns `nil` instead of an empty map,
causing callers that iterate over the result to panic. Fix the variable
extraction path in `regexp.go` to always set a non-nil map in the
request context.

relevant_preexisting_tests.reason: exercises the /static route (no path variables) calling Vars(r), directly covers setMatch and requestWithRouteAndVars on a route with no path variables
exercises router context population including route matching and context variable storage

test_selection.test_query: Find tests that verify the behavior of setMatch, requestWithVars, requestWithRouteAndVars in regexp.go and mux.go for routes without path variables

thrash_preventing_defs.name: Vars
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: understanding what Vars() returns when varsKey is absent from context (returns nil) is necessary to understand why the empty map must reach the context

thrash_preventing_defs.name: extractVars
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: understanding that extractVars creates the map lazily (only when varsN non-empty) confirms why setMatch never initializes m.Vars for routes with no variables

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set covers only the three functions directly edited. The thrash_preventing set adds Vars() (so an agent understands why the empty map must reach context — without seeing Vars(), an agent might not realize the nil-vs-empty distinction matters) and extractVars (so an agent understands why setMatch never initializes the map for variable-free routes). Without these two, an agent would likely open them anyway to understand the data flow before making changes.
